title: Networking & Internet Infrastructure
subtitle: From cables to BGP — how the internet actually works
description: A practical networking course for engineers: OSI layers, TCP/IP, DNS, routing (BGP, OSPF), HTTP/2 and HTTP/3, NAT and CDNs, and the physical layer underneath. Optimized for understanding what happens when you type a URL — and how to debug when it doesn't.
audience: Engineers who want to understand the network beneath their apps. Not a CCNA cram course; designed to build mental models you'll use for years.
outcomes: Read the OSI / TCP/IP stack from physical layer up
Know how routing works (BGP, OSPF) at a working level
Diagnose network issues with tcpdump, dig, traceroute
Understand HTTP/2, HTTP/3 (QUIC), and why they exist
Design with CDNs, NAT, and load balancers
Reason about latency and bandwidth trade-offs

modules.title: The stack: from cables to packets

lessons.title: OSI and TCP/IP — two models, one network
Ethernet, MAC, and the physical layer
TCP vs UDP: connection-oriented and not

modules.title: DNS and the address book of the internet

lessons.title: Record types: A, AAAA, MX, CNAME, TXT
BGP: the protocol that runs the internet
QUIC and the future of transport
